subject: [RFC PATCH] mm: Avoiding split large folios if swap has no space

reviews.author: David (Arm)
reviews.summary:
Reviewer questioned the necessity of avoiding split large folios if swap has no space, citing that the remaining headroom is less than a large folio and suggesting a fuzz factor around the limit is acceptable

Reviewer questioned whether implementing a more complex solution is worth it, given the remaining headroom is less than a large folio and temporary overcharging in other places is acceptable.
reviews.raw_body:
I was asking myself the same, but when we think in terms of THPs on arm64 64k
we're in the range of double-digit MiBs.

---

Thanks! @Barry, up to you if we want to implement that right away or if we're
simply going to assume that if charging fails, not worth splitting (changing the
existing handling IIUC).

-- 
Cheers,

David

2026-06-25.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory cgroup charging mechanism to avoid splitting large folios when there is no available swap space. The current implementation tries to charge against the first ancestor that fails, but this can lead to unnecessary splits. Instead, the patch proposes calculating the actual headroom by walking up the hierarchy and taking the minimum of all ancestors' available swap space. This approach aims to relax the strict limit enforcement in favor of a more flexible margin-based calculation.

reviews.author: Barry Song
reviews.summary:
Reviewer questioned the necessity of the patch, suggesting that a fuzz factor around the limit is acceptable and noting that the remaining headroom is less than a large folio.

Reviewer Barry Song noted that the current implementation may incur significant latency for I/O or compression, and suggested splitting huge folios into 2MB folios instead of base pages when swapping out to allow architectures with large folios to use THP_SWAP.

Reviewer pointed out that the patch's approach is similar to what mem_cgroup_get_nr_swap_pages() already does, suggesting that the effort may not be worth it given the limited headroom.

Reviewer Barry Song suggested modifying the __mem_cgroup_try_charge_swap() function to return an additional argument for the remaining swap space, which would be used in conjunction with mem_cgroup_margin() math to determine headroom. He proposed adding a fuzz factor around the limit and provided a code concept that includes this modification.
sentiment_signals: questioning the value of the patch

2026-06-26.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's memory cgroup charging mechanism to avoid splitting large folios when there is no available swap space. The current implementation tries to charge against the first ancestor that fails, but this can lead to unnecessary splits. Instead, the patch proposes calculating the actual headroom by walking up the hierarchy and taking the minimum of all ancestors' available swap space. This approach aims to relax the strict limit enforcement in favor of a more flexible margin-based calculation.
